clarification_questions: Did Ford transfer the note with recourse or without recourse? Does Ford retain any continuing involvement or obligation if the maker defaults?

ambiguities.aspect: Whether discounting the note at the bank constitutes derecognition (sale) or a collateralized borrowing

flags.aspect: Determining whether note discounting meets IFRS 9 derecognition criteria

ambiguities.aspect: Whether discounting the note at the bank constitutes derecognition (sale) or a collateralized borrowing
ambiguities.clarification_question: Did Ford transfer the note with recourse or without recourse? Does Ford retain any continuing involvement or obligation if the maker defaults?

output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford received a non-interest-bearing note with face value $100,000 in settlement of a trade receivable. After 50 days, Ford discounted the note at the bank at 15% annual discount rate. The bank discount is calculated on the remaining 40 days (90 - 50 = 40 days) of the note's term. Discount = $100,000 × 0.15 × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. However, the accounting treatment depends critically on whether this is a derecognition (sale without recourse) or a collateralized borrowing (with recourse). Under derecognition: Dr Cash $98,356.16, Dr Loss on Note Discounting $1,643.84, Cr Notes Receivable $100,000. Under collateralized borrowing: Dr Cash $98,356.16, Cr Notes Payable to Bank $98,356.16 (with the original note remaining on books). The transaction text does not specify recourse terms or continuing involvement, making this a material ambiguity that prevents completion of the entry.
